Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on December 17, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ADELIA E. INGRAM LIVNG TRUST OF 2013 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demands And Form Interrogatory And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] The tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Petitioner and Trustee Carlyce Ingram's ("Petitioner") request to continue this hearing is DENIED. Objectors DeAries Ingram and Adrienne Ingram' Motion to Compel Further Responses to Inspection Demands and Form Interrogatory is GRANTED. Objector served proper discovery. By their motion, Objectors have shown that Petitioner failed to provide Code-compliant responses to inspection demands ## 67 and 68 in that his responses amounted to unmeritorious and/or too general objections and/or representations of an inability to comply that were inadequate or evasive. (See CCP section 2031.310, subds. (a)(2) & (3); see also Probate Code section 1000.) Objectors have shown that Petitioner failed to provide Code-compliant responses to form interrogatory 17.1 as to denied requests for admission 1, 7, and 11-24 in that the responses were evasive and incomplete (actually, non-existent). (See CCP section 2030.300, subd. (a)(1); see also Probate Code section 1000.) The motion was served on Petitioner's former counsel Elaine Lee and Petitioner's current counsel David Simon and the motion implicates the discovery conduct of both counsel. Former counsel Elaine Lee filed no opposition. Through Simon, Petitioner filed an opposition to the motion to compel but presented no argument defending his responses or lack thereof, other than insufficient time. Petitioner has not justified his responses/failures to respond. Petitioner is ordered to provide the required responses within 10 days of the date notice of entry of this order is served upon him. Objectors' request for sanctions is GRANTED. Sanctions are awarded under CCP sections 2023.010 and 2023.030, subd. (a). Petitioner's failure to respond as required to inspection demands ## 67 and 68 amounts to a misuse of the discover process, as the August 31 responses and subsequent failure to produce documents or otherwise respond constituted unmeritorious objection to discovery without substantial justification and evasive responses to discovery. (CCP section 2023.010 subds. (e), and (f).) Petitioner's failure to respond as required to form interrogatory 17.1 as to denied requests for admission 1, 7, and 11-24, amounts to a misuse of the discover process, as the August 31 responses constitute evasive responses to an authorized method of discovery and a failure to respond to an authorized method of discovery. (CCP section 2023.010 subds. (d), and (f). There is no showing that Petitioner made any attempt to provide further responses to any of the subject discovery in the two-and-a-half months between the filing of the motion and the hearing on this motion. Sanctions are awarded under CCP sections 2031.310, subd (h) and 2030.300, subd. (d), based on the unsuccessful opposition to the motion and Petitioner's failure to show that imposition of the sanctions would be unjust. The court finds that former counsel Lee and current counsel Simon are responsible for the misuses of the discovery process, with Lee being 2/3rds responsible and Simon 1/3rd. Former counsel Lee shall pay to Objectors $1,840 within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order and current counsel Simon shall pay to Objectors $920 within 10 days of service of notice of entry of entry of this order (for a total sanctions award of $2,760). Objectors shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. [Part 1 of 2]
